steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The conclusion is that Great Britain had a greater number of damaging fungus species than either Switzerland or Hungary. Supporting data must state Great Britain's fungus count and show it exceeds both other countries' fungus counts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Hungary reported 18 damaging fungus species but only 13 damaging insect species.': Only mentions Hungary and compares fungus to insects within Hungary — never references Great Britain. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Great Britain reported 290 damaging fungus species, whereas Switzerland reported 78 damaging insect species.': Compares Great Britain's fungus count to Switzerland's insect count — different categories, not a valid comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Great Britain reported 290 damaging fungus species, which is more than either Switzerland or Hungary reported.': States Great Britain's fungus count and explicitly confirms it exceeds both Switzerland's and Hungary's fungus counts. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Switzerland and Hungary reported 11 and 1 damaging fungus species, respectively, which is far fewer than Great Britain reported.': References both other countries and implies Great Britain is higher, but never states Great Britain's actual count — the data cited focuses on the other countries rather than directly establishing Great Britain's figure. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
